metadata.source_paper: Faturrakhman, M. L., Sanjaya, I., Jamal, J., Saputro, D. H., & Setiawan, R. (2025). Tectonic geomorphology analysis in natural hydrogen exploration: A case study from Tanjung Api, Sulawesi. Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. https://doi.org/10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
metadata.region: Tanjung Api — Ampana — Central Sulawesi (Tomini Bay coast, East Arm)
metadata.comprehension_summary: Tectonic geomorphology (IRTA = Mountain Front Sinuosity + Basin Shape Index + Drainage Density) applied to Tanjung Api/Ampana area to support natural hydrogen exploration. IRTA class 1–2 (high–very high tectonic activity) concentrated around Ampana and Toili faults. Two H2 seep localities identified: Tanjung Api coast (>1000 ppm, 1000–1400 m³/day) and Pulodalagan hot spring (144–197 ppm). H2 generation via serpentinization of ESO ultramafics, with active faults as water infiltration conduits.
main_conclusions: Tanjung Api coast H2 seep: flow rate 1000–1400 m³/day, H2 > 1000 ppm — among highest natural H2 flow rates reported globally
Pulodalagan hot spring H2: 144–197 ppm from travertine crack, co-located with Class 1–2 tectonic activity
Both H2 seeps spatially coincide with high–very high tectonic activity zones (IRTA Class 1–2) and are located along the Ampana and Toili active faults
Active faults act as dual conduit: water infiltration pathway into ultramafics (triggers serpentinization) + fluid migration pathway to surface
Research area composed mainly of Bongka Formation (early–late Pliocene, ophiolite-rich conglomerate) and ultramafics — both susceptible to serpentinization
Tectonic geomorphology validated as exploration tool for subsurface natural hydrogen targets

faturrakhman_2025_tanjung_api.apa: Faturrakhman, M. L., Sanjaya, I., Jamal, J., Saputro, D. H., & Setiawan, R. (2025). Tectonic geomorphology analysis in natural hydrogen exploration: A case study from Tanjung Api, Sulawesi. Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. https://doi.org/10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
faturrakhman_2025_tanjung_api.topic: Tectonic geomorphology, natural hydrogen exploration, Tanjung Api

properties.name: H2 seep Tanjung Api — >1000 ppm
properties.full_name: Natural hydrogen seep — Tanjung Api coast, Central Sulawesi (Faturrakhman et al. 2025)
properties.host_rock: ESO ultramafics + terrace deposits (jointed Quaternary limestone)
properties.source_apa: Faturrakhman et al. (2025). Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. doi:10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
properties.paper_finding: H2 >1000 ppm, flow rate 1000–1400 m³/day. Gas bubbles emerging from underwater seeps and from soil along jointed terrace deposits. Located in IRTA Class 1–2 zone along Ampana and Toili faults.
properties.extraction_notes: Tanjung Api cape, Central Sulawesi. Coordinates estimated from geographic position of Tanjung Api promontory (Tomini Bay coast, ~8 km N of Ampana town) and Fig. 1/Fig. 2 in paper. ±3 km precision.

conclusions.text: Tanjung Api H2 seep: >1000 ppm H2, flow rate 1000–1400 m³/day — one of the highest natural H2 flow rates documented. Located at Class 1–2 tectonic activity zone along active Ampana/Toili faults.
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La serpentinización activa del ESO en Tanjung Api está controlada por fallas activas que facilitan la infiltración de agua — el H2 natural es marcador de actividad sísmica y de circulación de fluidos en la zona de colisión ESO/Banggai-Sula

properties.name: H2 seep Pulodalagan — termal spring 144-197 ppm
properties.full_name: Natural hydrogen seep — Pulodalagan hot spring, near Tanjung Api (Faturrakhman et al. 2025)
properties.source_apa: Faturrakhman et al. (2025). Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. doi:10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
properties.paper_finding: H2 = 144–197 ppm from crack in travertine deposits. Hot spring with periodic gas bubbling. Located in IRTA Class 1–2 zone along Ampana fault.
properties.extraction_notes: Pulodalagan is described as near Tanjung Api coast. Coordinates estimated ~2–3 km SE of Tanjung Api seep based on Fig. 2 relative position. ±5 km precision — exact location not given in text.

conclusions.text: Pulodalagan hot spring H2: 144–197 ppm from travertine crack; periodic gas bubbling. Co-located with Class 1–2 tectonic activity, confirming spatial correlation between active faulting and H2 seepage.
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Las termas con H2 en Pulodalagan indican circulación profunda de fluidos hidrotermales a través de fracturas en la zona de falla — consistente con el modelo de serpentinización controlada por fallas activas del ESO

properties.name: Área estudio Tanjung Api — geomorfología tectónica
properties.full_name: Research area — Tanjung Api / Ampana tectonic geomorphology study (Faturrakhman et al. 2025)
properties.irta_high_zones: Class 1-2 along Ampana and Toili faults
properties.source_apa: Faturrakhman et al. (2025). Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. doi:10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
properties.paper_finding: 30 mountain fronts + 52 sub-basins analyzed. IRTA Class 1–2 (very high–high tectonic activity) dominates the western and eastern parts; Class 2–3 scattered throughout. Area composed of ESO ultramafics and Pliocene Bongka Formation (ophiolite-rich conglomerate).
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono estimado del área de estudio a partir de Fig. 1 (mapa de localización) y descripción textual. ±5 km de precisión en los bordes.

conclusions.text: IRTA dominantly Class 2–3 with Class 1–2 concentrated along Ampana and Toili faults — high tectonic activity corroborates active faulting control on H2 seepage
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La zona de Tanjung Api/Ampana es una región de actividad tectónica alta sostenida, resultado de la colisión ESO/Banggai-Sula activa y de las fallas sinistral-NW-SE que la acomodan

properties.name: Ampana Fault — NW-SE strike-slip
properties.full_name: Ampana Fault — NW-SE active fault, Tanjung Api area (sensu Rusmana et al. 1993, documented by Faturrakhman et al. 2025)
properties.source_apa: Faturrakhman et al. (2025). Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. doi:10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
properties.paper_finding: Ampana Fault identified as key structural control on H2 seepage in Tanjung Api. Located in IRTA Class 1–2 zone. Triangular facets and linear valleys observed along fault trace (field observation).
properties.extraction_notes: Traza inferred a partir de Fig. 2 y posición geográfica de Ampana. La traza exacta proviene del mapa geológico Rusmana et al. (1993), no digitalizada directamente en este paper. Tendencia NW-SE aproximada inferida de contexto textual y geología regional. ±10 km precisión.

conclusions.text: Ampana Fault acts as conduit for water infiltration into ESO ultramafics, triggering serpentinization and H2 generation — confirmed by co-location of H2 seeps with Class 1–2 tectonic activity
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La Falla Ampana es un conducto activo para fluidos en la zona ESO — su actividad sísmica y la infiltración de agua controlada por la falla son el mecanismo directo de generación de H2 natural en Tanjung Api

properties.name: Toili Fault — NW-SE active fault
properties.full_name: Toili Fault — NW-SE active fault, southern Tanjung Api area (sensu Rusmana et al. 1993, documented by Faturrakhman et al. 2025)
properties.source_apa: Faturrakhman et al. (2025). Riset Geologi dan Pertambangan, 35(1), 37–48. doi:10.55981/risetgeotam.2025.1357
properties.paper_finding: Toili Fault co-located with H2 seepage zones and IRTA Class 1–2 activity in the study area. Active fault morphology confirmed by field observation (triangular facets, linear valleys).
properties.extraction_notes: Traza inferred a partir de Fig. 2 y posición de Toili town (~122.45°E, -1.18°S). Tendencia NW-SE aproximada inferida del contexto. La traza exacta proviene del mapa de Rusmana et al. (1993). ±15 km precisión — baja confianza geométrica.

conclusions.text: Toili Fault is co-located with high tectonic activity (IRTA Class 1–2) and H2 seepage, confirming active fault role in hydrogen generation via serpentinization conduit
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El sistema Ampana-Toili de fallas NW-SE controla la generación y migración de H2 en la zona de Tanjung Api — estructuralmente vinculadas a la colisión activa ESO/Banggai-Sula
